title: Erik Killmonger
content:
{{Otheruses|Black Panther}} {{CharHub|Erik Killmonger|N'Jadaka}} {{Character |image = Black Panther Textless Character Poster 03.jpg |real name = N'Jadaka (Wakandan name){{Ref|BP}}
Erik Stevens (legal name){{Ref|BP}} |alias = {{Alias |codenames = Erik Killmonger{{Ref|BP}}
Black Panther{{Ref|BP}} |derivatives = E{{Ref|BP}}}} |species = [[Human]] |citizenship = {{Citizenship|USA}}
{{Citizenship|WKD}} |affiliation = {{Affiliation|USNA}} (formerly)

*{{Affiliation|SEAL}} (formerly)
[[Golden Tribe]]
[[Tribal Council]] |gender = Male |DOD = [[2016|Summer 2016]]{{Ref|BP}}{{Ref|MSTMCUAOT}} |title = King of [[Wakanda]]
[[:Category:Black Panthers|Black Panther]] |movie = ''[[Black Panther (film)|Black Panther]]''
''[[Black Panther: Wakanda Forever]]'' |tv series = ''[[Eyes of Wakanda]]'' (1 episode) |actor = [[Michael B. Jordan]]
[[Seth Carr]] (young) |status = Deceased}} {{Quote|I know how colonizers think. So we're gonna use their own strategy against 'em. We're gonna send [[vibranium]] weapons out to our [[War Dogs]]. They'll arm oppressed people all over the [[Earth|world]] so they can finally rise up and kill those in power. And their children. And anyone else who takes their side.|Erik Killmonger to the [[Tribal Council]]|Black Panther (film)}} '''N'Jadaka''', legally named '''Erik Stevens''', was a former United States [[United States Navy SEALs|Navy SEAL]] and the son of the [[Wakanda|Wakandan]] Prince [[N'Jobu]]. When N'Jadaka was a child, his uncle [[T'Chaka]] [[Murder of N'Jobu|killed]] his father for trying to murder [[Zuri]] for outing him after he had [[Attack on Wakanda|aided]] [[Ulysses Klaue]] in stealing [[vibranium]] from Wakanda. This drove N'Jadaka to [[Erik Killmonger's Revolution|dedicate his life]] to forcibly dethrone his cousin, [[T'Challa]], in order to finally accomplish his father's plan to seize control of the world by using all of Wakanda's [[vibranium]] technology to cease the oppression of people of African descent all across the world. In order to achieve this, N'Jadaka joined the Navy Seals and was eventually assigned to a black-ops unit, where his numerous confirmed kills earned him the nickname '''Erik Killmonger'''. In [[2016]], following T'Chaka's death, Killmonger allied with Klaue, only to then kill him to gain access into Wakanda. Challenging for his bloodright, Killmonger murdered Zuri and seemingly killed T'Challa during their ritual combat and claimed the throne, becoming '''Black Panther''', ensuring he would be the last of the lineage by burning the ancient [[Heart-Shaped Herb]]s. Shortly before Killmonger unleashed his plans, T'Challa returned and challenged Killmonger, eventually defeating and killing him during their [[Battle of Mount Bashenga|final battle]], though his death inspired T'Challa to stop isolating Wakanda from the world and provide other nations with their resources. [[2025|Nine years later]], Killmonger communicated with [[Shuri]] when she visited the [[Ancestral Plane]], informing her that they were very much alike with their mutual desire for revenge following the murder of a family member. Despite Killmonger's advice that she follow through with her vengeance against [[Namor]], Shuri ultimately managed to evade succumbing to it. ==Biography== ===Early Life=== ====Living in the United States==== {{Quote|Your [[N'Jobu|uncle]] took a [[War Dogs|War Dog]] assignment in [[United States of America|America]]. Your [[T'Chaka|father]] placed me there to observe. Unbeknownst to him, your uncle fell in love with an [[Erik Killmonger's Mother|American woman]]. They had a child. The hardships he saw there radicalized your uncle.|[[Zuri]] to [[T'Challa]]|Black Panther (film)}} Erik Stevens was born in [[Oakland]], [[California]] as the son of [[Wakanda]]n Prince [[N'Jobu]] and an [[Erik Killmonger's Mother|American woman]]. In addition to his legal American name, his father also gave him the Wakandan name N'Jadaka. His mother was eventually incarcerated, leaving N'Jobu to raise his son alone. N'Jobu told his son about Wakanda and its true nature and promised the young boy that he would one day take him to Wakanda, claiming that the sunsets here were the most beautiful in the [[Earth|world]]. During his youth, N'Jadaka found his father's book which featured many secrets from Wakanda, including N'Jobu's own [[Wakandan Royal Ring]] and the way to enter the secret country.{{Ref|BP}} ====Death of His Father==== [[File:N'Jadaka (1992).png|{{AltFloat}}|Stevens spots a [[Royal Talon Fighter]]]] {{Quote|Everybody dies. It's just life around [[Oakland|here]].|Erik Killmonger to [[N'Jobu]]|Black Panther (film)}} In [[1992]], [[N'Jobu]] planned to share the weapons of [[Wakanda]] with the rest of the [[Earth|world]] so the African Americans who had so little power could rise up and fight back. To achieve this goal, N'Jobu made an alliance with a mercenary named [[Ulysses Klaue]] to get into Wakanda and steal their [[vibranium]] deposits. Klaue's actions resulted in the deaths of several Wakandans, but Stevens was unaware of this and continued life as normal. [[File:BP - Erik Discovers His Father's Death.png|{{AltFloat}}|Stevens finds [[N'Jobu|father]]'s dead body]] Unbeknownst to Stevens, [[T'Chaka]] learned about his brother's treachery from his friend James, who was secretly T'Chaka's confidant [[Zuri]], and had gained the trust of N'Jobu to learn of his plans against Wakanda. T'Chaka went to [[Oakland]] and confronted his brother at his apartment just as N'Jobu was formulating a plan{{Ref|BP}} to free Stevens' mother,{{Cite}} threatening to return him to Wakanda to face punishment. In response, N'Jobu attempted to kill Zuri. T'Chaka retaliated by impaling N'Jobu with his [[Panther Habit]]'s claws, killing N'Jobu. [[File:BP - Erik Holds N'Jobu.png|{{AltFloat}}|Stevens cries and holds his [[N'Jobu|father]]'s corpse]] Unaware of his father's death, Stevens was playing basketball with his friends until he saw the [[Royal Talon Fighter]] above their apartment complex. He rushed inside, only to find his father lying dead on the floor with panther claws in his chest. Devastated over the death of his father, Stevens had held his father's body and cried, before recovering his father's diary with his [[Wakandan Royal Ring]] as well as everything Stevens ever needed to know about Wakanda.{{Ref|BP}} Shortly after, Stevens' mother died while in prison,{{Cite}} leaving him as an orphan on the streets without no family or support.{{Ref|BP}} ===[[Erik Killmonger's Revolution|Seeking Revenge]]=== ====Becoming Killmonger==== [[File:Killmonger-MilitaryFile.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Stevens becomes a highly skilled warrior]] {{Quote|Started calling him Killmonger. He joined a {{WPS|Joint Special Operations Command|JSOC}} ghost unit. Now, these guys are serious. They will drop off the grid, so they can commit assassinations and take down governments.|[[Everett Ross]]|Black Panther (film)}} Growing up with top marks in school, Stevens went on to attend and graduate from the [[United States Naval Academy]] in [[Annapolis]] and the [[Massachusetts Institute of Technology]], before joining the [[United States Navy SEALs]]. Within the SEAL teams, Stevens was deployed to [[Afghanistan]], [[Iraq]] and [[Africa]], where he had the highest count of confirmed kills amongst everyone from his unit. Stevens also participated in several stateside missions in the [[United States of America]]. Eventually, Stevens was recruited into a {{WPS|Joint Special Operations Command}} ghost unit associated with the [[CIA]] as a highly skilled black ops mercenary. Operating within the JSOC unit, he went off the grid in order to commit assassinations and take down foreign governments on behalf of the United States. As the highly skilled soldier, Stevens was responsible for eliminating dozens of hostiles, cutting his skin with tribal {{WPS|Scarification|Crocodile Scarring}}, with one for each kill. Due to his ruthlessness, while serving within the military, Stevens eventually gained the nickname "Killmonger."{{Ref|BP}} ====Working with Ulysses Klaue==== =====[[Robbery of the Museum of Great Britain|Museum of Great Britain Robbery]]===== [[File:Black Panther OCT17 Trailer 48.png|{{AltFloat}}|Killmonger looks at all the [[Africa]]n artifacts]] {{Quote|Don't trip, I'mma take it off your hands for you."
"These items aren't for sale."
"How do you think your ancestors got these? You think they paid a fair price? Or did they take it like they took everything else.|Erik Killmonger and [[Fiona Thomas]]|Black Panther (film)}} Seeking to enact his plan to return to [[Wakanda]], Killmonger struck a deal with [[Ulysses Klaue]], who previously worked with his father and had his own history with Wakanda. Together, they had discovered a [[vibranium]] weapon which was being shown in the [[Museum of Great Britain]]. Killmonger and Klaue made a plan to steal the weapon for themselves, as Killmonger went to investigate personally by going undercover. [[File:KillmongerLookingAtAxe.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Killmonger speaks with [[Fiona Thomas]]]] While looking at the [[Africa]]n masks that were on display, Killmonger was joined by [[Fiona Thomas]], claiming to have an interest in their history. As Killmonger pointed to each mask, Thomas was able to explain where each one came from. Finally, Killmonger pointed to the [[Vibranium Ax]], which Thomas claimed was from [[Benin]]'s {{WPS|Fula people|Fula Tribe}}, only for Killmonger to correct her and explain that it was actually from Wakanda. [[File:Killmonger-ItsFromWakanda.png|{{AltFloat}}|Killmonger explains the axe's origin]] Killmonger then claimed he would take the axe from them, confusing Thomas who noted the items were not for sale, to which Killmonger noted that Thomas' ancestors had stolen all the artifacts like many others from African history. As Thomas then tried to get him to leave, Killmonger had revealed that she was drinking coffee that had been poisoned by [[Linda (Mercenary)|Linda]], just as Thomas then collapsed onto the ground in pain, causing security to ignore Killmonger and run to her aid. [[File:Killmonger-WhydYouNotShootHimHere.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Killmonger and [[Ulysses Klaue|Klaue]] kill the security]] As Killmonger called out for aid and feigned concern for Thomas, Klaue and [[Limbani]] arrived on the scene disguised as ambulance workers and proceeded to execute Thomas and two of the security guards, all while Linda had hacked into the security cameras to ensure they were not being watched. Killmonger then watched as Klaue told the final security guard he could leave, before shooting him anyway, as Klaue explained to Killmonger that spreading out the crime scene made them look like amateurs. [[File:KlaueKillmongerStealWeapon.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Killmonger working alongside [[Ulysses Klaue]]]] Killmonger and Klaue then stepped over to the vibranium axe on display, as Klaue used his [[Ulysses Klaue's Prosthetic Arm|prosthetic arm]] to shatter all the glass and take it, vibrating the axe so all the rust fell off as Klaue confirmed that this was indeed vibranium, telling Killmonger that they would soon be rich. Killmonger recommended that Klaue sell the axe sooner rather than later, to which Klaue explained that he already had a [[Everett Ross|buyer]] while he and Limbani packed the axe away and prepared to leave. [[File:KillmongerLookingAtAfricanMask.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Killmonger decides to steal an [[Africa]]n mask]] With Klaue and Limbani packing up, Killmonger noted that whatever they did, the Wakandans would have likely showed up to try and stop them, to which Klaue simply expressed his excitement at the chance of killing more Wakandans while making his profit. Killmonger, however, then became distracted as he looked at a [[Dogon Mask]] on display, telling Klaue that while the mask was not made of vibranium, he liked the design and therefore stole it for his personal use. [[File:KillmongerKissesLinda.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Killmonger escapes with his crew]] Having gotten what they came for, Killmonger and the rest of his crew made their escape, as Klaue and Limbani put Killmonger on the stretcher and wheeled him out to their ambulance where Linda was waiting. Once out of sight, Killmonger sat up and kissed Linda while Klaue shut the door and Limbani drove them away. As the next stage of their plan went ahead, Killmonger stayed out of sight as Klaue prepared to sell the axe to his buyer for a suitcase full of diamonds.{{Ref|BP}} ====Rescue of Ulysses Klaue==== [[File:Black Panther OCT17 Trailer 34.png|{{AltFloat}}|Killmonger prepares to save [[Ulysses Klaue]]]] {{Quote|There was a man today, wearing a [[Wakandan Royal Ring|ring]] identical to this one."
"That is not possible."
"He helped [[Ulysses Klaue|Klaue]] escape from us and he was wearing this ring, my [[Azzuri|grandfather]]'s ring. Do not tell me what is possible.|[[T'Challa]] and [[Zuri]]|Black Panther (film)}} Unsurprisingly to Killmonger, [[Ulysses Klaue]]'s attempt at selling their stolen [[vibranium]] axe to the [[CIA]] resulted with him being captured due to the interference of the [[Wakanda]]ns. With Klaue being interrogated by the CIA, Killmonger then regrouped with [[Linda (Mercenary)|Linda]] and [[Limbani]] as they prepared to get Klaue free, with Killmonger wearing a Bulletproof Vest and the [[Dogon Mask]] which he had previously stolen. [[File:BP Teaser Trailer 27.png|{{AltFloat}}|Killmonger blows open the wall to the base]] While Linda hacked into the CIA's security cameras to ensure they were not seen, Killmonger and Limbani then took position outside of the [[CIA South Korean Black Site|Facility]] as Killmonger planted explosives against the wall where Klaue was being held. Before anybody inside the facility could even realize that something was going wrong, Killmonger detonated the explosives, blowing down the wall while Klaue yelled out in hysterical excitement as he realized that his allies had come to his rescue. [[File:KillmongerShootsAtCIAAgents.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Killmonger shooting at [[Everett Ross]]' agents]] Arming himself, Killmonger immediately began firing into the facility at the agents, shooting [[Everett Ross]] directly in the spine as Ross had tried to save [[Nakia]] from Killmonger's bullets. While Killmonger continued firing into the base, Limbani and Linda picked up the still laughing Klaue and carried him and the chair he was handcuffed to into their van, as Killmonger then threw his grenade into the facility to ensure that nobody could follow them as they were escaping. [[File:KillmongerRescuingKlaue.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Killmonger takes [[Ulysses Klaue]] away]] However, as Killmonger backed away to the van while still shooting into the facility, the [[T'Challa|Black Panther]] charged after him, with Killmonger shooting with a grenade launcher and knocking the Panther back, absorbing the impact from his [[Panther Habit]]. Killmonger then got onto the van while his crew drove away, locking eyes with Black Panther who had spotted his [[Wakandan Royal Ring]], all while having successfully rescued Klaue from custody.{{Ref|BP}} ====[[Assassination of Ulysses Klaue|Betraying Ulysses Klaue]]==== [[File:BP - Klaue Leaving For Wakanda.png|{{AltFloat}}|Killmonger and [[Ulysses Klaue]] prepare to go]] {{Quote|I don't worry about no brand. Check these out. Each one is for a kill."
"You can scar yourself as much as you like. To [[Wakanda|them]], you'll just be an outsider. You're crazy to think that you can just walk in there.|Erik Killmonger and [[Ulysses Klaue]]|Black Panther (film)}} Having made their escape, Killmonger and [[Ulysses Klaue]] prepared to leave [[South Korea]], as Killmonger then asked Klaue if he had always sold his weapons to the [[CIA]]. While they were loading all their equipment on board the plane to leave, Klaue promised to ensure that Killmonger got paid, as Killmonger then requested that Klaue drop him off in [[Wakanda]], only to be warned against doing so. [[File:KillmongerKillsLinda.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Killmonger aims at [[Ulysses Klaue]] and [[Linda (Mercenary)|Linda]]]] However, Killmonger insisted that he needed to go to Wakanda and proceeded to shoot and kill [[Limbani]], while Klaue grabbed [[Linda (Mercenary)|Linda]] and held her at gunpoint, demanding that Killmonger drop his weapon, or he would kill his girlfriend. As a tense standoff then began, Linda had apologized before Killmonger reassured her that everything would be okay, and then proceeded to shoot and kill her, wounding Klaue who then made his escape attempt while Killmonger followed him. [[File:KillmongerShootingKlaue.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Killmonger mortally wounding [[Ulysses Klaue]]]] While Klaue desperately ran across the airfield in an attempt to get to safety, Killmonger remained close behind as the two former allies waited for their opportunity to kill the other. When Klaue then spotted Killmonger stepping into sight, he stood up from his hiding place and fired at him, only for Killmonger to shoot back and hit Klaue directly in the chest, causing him to fall to his back in agony and drop his gun, as Killmonger walked over to the mortally wounded Klaue. [[File:Assassinationofulyssesklaue.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Killmonger stands by the dying [[Ulysses Klaue|Klaue]]]] As Killmonger stood over the wounded Klaue and kicked his gun away, he listened as Klaue questioned his desire to go to Wakanda, pointing to the brand on his neck and claiming that it was what the Wakandans did to people like them, only for Killmonger to show off all of his scars he gave himself during his time in the [[United States Navy SEALs]], noting that each of one of them had been for a kill. However, Klaue noted that he would never rule and still be seen as an outsider. [[File:BP - Killmonger (Mouth Branded).png|{{AltFloat}}|Killmonger reveals his [[Wakanda]]n identity]] With Klaue insisting he could never get in Wakanda, Killmonger then responded by revealing his own secret [[War Dogs]] tattoo under his lip, which [[N'Jobu]] had given him as a child. Seeing this, Klaue realized Killmonger was actually Wakandan and began laughing, noting that he had assumed Killmonger was just another crazy [[United States of America|American]]. While Klaue continued to laugh out loud, Killmonger raised his gun and shot him once again, finally killing Klaue once and for all.{{Ref|BP}} ====Meeting his Relatives==== [[File:Black Panther (film) 103.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Killmonger demands to meet [[T'Challa]]]] {{Quote|Y'all sitting up here comfortable. Must feel good. There's about two billion people all over the [[Earth|world]] that looks like us and their lives are a lot harder. [[Wakanda]] has the tools to liberate 'em all.|Erik Killmonger to [[Tribal Council]]|Black Panther (film)}} Having successfully murdered [[Ulysses Klaue]], Killmonger then flew the plane to [[Africa]] and soon arrived in [[Wakanda]], with Klaue's corpse in tow. Killmonger then presented the body to [[W'Kabi]], the leader of the [[Border Tribe]] that protected Wakanda from any outsiders. W'Kabi was given the body to personally inspect. [[File:BP Teaser Trailer 43.png|{{AltFloat}}|Killmonger challenges [[T'Challa]]]] Amazed that an outsider had successfully killed their greatest enemy, W'Kabi brought Killmonger to meet with the [[Tribal Council]] where he was presented to [[T'Challa]], who was surrounded by his [[Dora Milaje]], who kept a close watch on Killmonger to ensure that he could not attempt to attack the King. Once invited to speak, Killmonger noted how he had served justice to Klaue who had been responsible for the previous [[Attack on Wakanda]] and had stolen [[vibranium]], noting that T'Challa had been unable to deliver this justice himself. [[File:Killmonger and T'Challa.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Killmonger is confronted by King [[T'Challa]]]] Angered by this insult, T'Challa got out of his throne and stepped up to Killmonger, closely followed by [[Okoye]], as T'Challa noted that he did not care that Killmonger had brought him Klaue's corpse, telling him that the only reason why he did not kill him where he stood was because he knew who Killmonger really was, as T'Challa questioned what Killmonger wanted, to which he claimed he wanted the throne, as [[Zawavari]] scoffed with all the other council members. However, Killmonger explained his reasoning, noting how Wakanda was keeping their technology from the black communities around the entire world that had been oppressed for hundreds of years. [[File:KillmongerExplainsMotives.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Killmonger explaining his own motivations]] Killmonger confirmed that he was referring to [[vibranium]] and all of the weapons that Wakanda produced, while T'Challa simply insisted that Wakanda's weapons would not be used to wage war against the rest of the world, although Killmonger claimed that the King of Wakanda should be responsible for all black communities that were being held down across the world. [[Ramonda]] declared that they had heard enough from Killmonger as she demanded that they reject his request for Wakanda's throne. [[File:Killmonger-AskMyName.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Killmonger tells [[T'Challa]] to ask for his name]] Refusing to back down, Killmonger suggested that they ask him who he really is, to which [[Shuri]] stepped up and claimed that Killmonger was merely a [[United States Navy SEALs|Navy SEAL]] named Erik Stevens, although Killmonger insisted that this was not his real name. T'Challa, however, refused to ask for Killmonger's name and had told the Dora Milaje to remove him, only for the [[River Tribe Elder]] to ask Killmonger the same question, revealing it to be N'Jadaka. [[File:Killmonger-RevealsFamilySecrets.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Killmonger reveals the truth about his past]] Speaking in Xhosa, N'Jadaka finally revealed [[N'Jobu]] was his father who had been [[Murder of N'Jobu|murdered]] by [[T'Chaka]] years before, and as Ramonda claimed that he was simply lying, W'Kabi confirmed what N'Jadaka was saying by revealing his [[Wakandan Royal Ring|Royal Ring]]. With a royal right, N'Jadaka demanded the chance to challenge T'Challa for the mantle of King and of the [[:Category:Black Panthers|Black Panther]], claiming that he would only need T'Challa to accept the challenge, which T'Challa reluctantly accepted.{{Ref|BP}} ====Duel for the Throne==== [[File:KillmongerRemovesVest.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Killmonger prepares himself for the fight]] {{Quote|I lived my entire life waitin' for this [[Coronation of Erik Killmonger|moment]]. I trained, I lied, I killed... just to get [[Wakanda|here]]. I killed in [[United States of America|America]], [[Afghanistan]], [[Iraq]]. I took life from my own brothers and sisters right here on this [[Africa|continent]]! And all this death... just so I could kill you.|Erik Killmonger to [[T'Challa]]|Black Panther (film)}} As they prepared for the duel, Killmonger and [[T'Challa]] were both taken to [[Warrior Falls]] where Killmonger was then given his spear and sword, choosing to break the spear in half so it was easier to strike with as the [[Tribal Council]] observed closely. Killmonger watched as [[Zuri]] stripped T'Challa of the Black Panther strength, leaving them on equal footing for the challenge, as Killmonger locked eyes with Zuri, who he remembered as [[N'Jobu]]'s friend and his uncle. [[File:AllThisDeathJustSoICouldKillYou.png|{{AltFloat}}|Killmonger vowing to kill [[T'Challa]] in the duel]] Once T'Challa stood back up, he gave Killmonger one final chance to lay down all his weapons so they could handle the situation another way. Instead, Killmonger then removed his Bulletproof Vest and shirt, revealing his tribal {{WPS|Scarification|Crocodile Scarring}}, and told T'Challa how he had lived his entire life for this one moment, noting how he had killed all across the world, including [[Afghanistan]], [[Iraq]] and [[Africa]], just so that he could come and have his chance at killing T'Challa. [[File:Black Panther5a8bb490f12c8.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Killmonger and [[T'Challa]] begin the duel]] With everything in place, Zuri had then announced that the challenge could begin as Killmonger furiously charged at T'Challa and began swinging his weapons at his enemy, with T'Challa being immediately on the defensive as Killmonger used every ounce of strength to land a deadly blow against him. All of Killmonger's [[United States Navy|military]] training allowed him to push T'Challa back, but he soon realized T'Challa's true skills with the tribal weapons as he managed to lock Killmonger into a block and held him back, using his shield to his advantage. [[File:Black Panther (film) 153.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Killmonger fights against [[T'Challa]]]] Becoming frustrated, Killmonger landed several heavy strikes against T'Challa, repeatedly striking his shield as he leaped through the air and attempt to beat T'Challa into submission. However, T'Challa was able to use all Killmonger's rage against him as he knocked him off balance and onto his back, threatening to kill him if Killmonger did not yield the duel. Killmonger, however, refused to back down once he got back onto his feet, as T'Challa continued gaining the advantage as he sliced Killmonger across the face with a non-fatal strike. [[File:KillmongerWoundingTChalla.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Killmonger wounding [[T'Challa]] several times]] Angered by T'Challa's advantage, Killmonger furiously continued fighting, as he was eventually able to land a heavy kick and managed to slice T'Challa across his inner thigh. While [[Shuri]] still shouted out encouragement to her brother, Killmonger continued beating him down before managing to slice him across the chest and arm, which caused T'Challa considerable pain. When T'Challa attempted to punch Killmonger, he responded by impaling him with his spear. [[File:KillmongerBlockedByZuri.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Killmonger's final strike is blocked by [[Zuri]]]] With T'Challa now mortally wounded, Killmonger was able to easily beat him down in front of his horrified family. Killmonger then raised his blade and prepared to execute the Wakandan King, telling him that it would be revenge for [[T'Chaka]] killing his own father. Before he could land the blow, Zuri stepped forward and blocked his blade with the [[Spear of Bashenga]] and knocking Killmonger's blade out of his hands. As everyone looked on, Zuri then confessed responsibility for the death of N'Jobu, farther angering Killmonger. [[File:KillmongerStandsOverTChalla.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Killmonger stands over the beaten [[T'Challa]]]] As Zuri told Killmonger to take his life over T'Challa's, Killmonger responded by declaring he would kill them both before he then grabbed the spear and stabbed Zuri in the heart. When the devastated T'Challa grabbed a sword and tried to strike back at Killmonger, he easily avoided the blow and questioned those watching if T'Challa was truly worthy of being King, before swiftly and brutally beating T'Challa into submission, much to the utter horror of everybody watching. [[File:KillmongerDefeatsTChalla.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Killmonger throws [[T'Challa]] off the waterfall]] Killmonger stood over T'Challa, who was now unable to stand as he called out to those watching and claimed that T'Challa would never be able to lead them into the future and was unworthy of holding the power of the [[:Category:Black Panthers|Black Panther]]. When T'Challa finally gathered enough strength to try and fight back, Killmonger responded by lifting him above his head before throwing him off the side of Warrior Falls, seemingly to his death while [[Ramonda]] then cried out in grief and horror. [[File:BP - Killmonger is Crowned as King.png|{{AltFloat}}|Killmonger is named as the King of [[Wakanda]]]] Standing victorious at the conclusion of the challenge with T'Challa, Killmonger told those watching that he was now their one true king, while [[Nakia]] pulled the devastated Ramonda and Shuri away to safety before Killmonger could have had them arrested to ensure his own power over Wakanda. Killmonger was then presented with the royal necklace before the [[Border Tribe]] and [[Dora Milaje]] saluted the newest King of Wakanda while Killmonger looked over his subjects.{{Ref|BP}} ===[[Erik Killmonger's Revolution|King of Wakanda]]=== ====Burning the Herb Garden==== [[File:BP - Killmonger (Purple Glow).png|{{AltFloat}}|Killmonger drinks the [[Heart-Shaped Herb]]]] {{Quote|Yeah, go ahead and burn all [[Heart-Shaped Herb|that]]."
"My king, we cannot do that, it is our tradition..."
"When I tell you to do something, I mean that shit. Burn it all!|Erik Killmonger and [[Sope]]|Black Panther (film)}} As per tradition, Killmonger entered the [[City of the Dead]] and underwent the ritual, where all Kings of [[Wakanda]] must go through by drinking the fluid of the [[Heart-Shaped Herb|heart-shaped herb]] and speaking with his ancestor's spirit within the [[Ancestral Plane]], becoming the Black Panther. The shaman, [[Sope]], ordered the others to bury Killmonger after the herb's fluid started to take effect. [[File:BP - Killmonger (After Looking Into N'Jobu's Stuff).png|{{AltFloat}}|Killmonger talks to his [[N'Jobu|father]]]] Once Killmonger was buried in the sand, he woke up in his [[N'Jobu's Safehouse|apartment]] from [[Oakland]]. Killmonger looked over the windows and looked suspiciously at the plane outside. Killmonger walked over to the apartment guest room and ripped apart the wall, seeing a book. Killmonger observed the book, looking over Wakandan literature and found his necklace attached to a [[Wakandan Royal Ring|ring]]. Killmonger donned the necklace and was happy to have it back again. [[File:Killmonger-MaybeYourHomeIsLost.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Killmonger tells [[N'Jobu]] about his home]] Interrupted by his [[N'Jobu|father]], Killmonger was told again by him to not look over his stuff without permission. N'Jobu then smiled at Killmonger and asked what he found, Killmonger responded by saying that he was looking for his home. N'Jobu was saving his secret in hopes he would find his home but was afraid that they would not take him in. Killmonger retorted by telling his father that maybe his home was lost and that was why they could not find him and his father. [[File:Black Panther OCT17 Trailer 52.png|{{AltFloat}}|Killmonger orders the herbs to be burned]] After waking up from the ancestral plane, Killmonger was told to calm down and brought to the center of the room to breathe. Killmonger asked Sope what he just experienced, only to be told that it was a ritual burial and planted each herb on the ground for other future kings. Killmonger questioned Sope about another King and responded that if there was another King to undergo the ritual burial, they would be ready. Killmonger ordered the herb garden to be burned, only to be told that they could not, for the sake of tradition. [[File:Black Panther OCT17 Trailer 46.png|{{AltFloat}}|Killmonger observing the [[Heart-Shaped Herb|herbs]] burning]] Frustrated with Sope, Killmonger ferociously grabbed her by the throat and lifted her off her feet. As he choked her, Killmonger noted that when he told her to do something, he meant it. Letting go of Sope, Killmonger ordered the rest of the shamans to burn all of the herbs in order to cement his status as king over Wakanda permanently and to prevent anyone from ever opposing him. As the shaman people burned the garden, Killmonger observed the landscape to make sure all of them were burned down.{{Ref|BP}} ====Seated in Power==== [[File:DidahqBU0AAHYPW.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Killmonger walks over to the [[Tribal Council]]]] {{Quote|You know, [[United States of America|where]] I'm from, when black folks started revolutions, they never had the firepower or the resources to fight their oppressors. Where was [[Wakanda]]? Yeah, all that ends today. We got spies embedded in every nation on Earth.|Erik Killmonger to the [[Tribal Council]]|Black Panther (film)}} In the next day of his reign, Killmonger entered the [[Tribal Council]], walking towards the throne and sitting down on it, breathing heavily for his accomplishment. Once the tribal leaders sat down afterward, Killmonger talked about where he was from, African Americans started a revolution but never had the firepower or the resources to defend themselves from their oppressors and questioned where [[Wakanda]] was when they needed them. [[File:BP - Killmonger Sitting In the Throne.png|{{AltFloat}}|Killmonger discusses his next step]] Farthering the discussion of his next court of action on ruling [[Wakanda]], Killmonger told the Elders that all of the oppression that African Americans faced would end from his ruling. Using examples, Killmonger expressed that spies were embedded in every nation and should know how colonizers would think, using their own strategy against them. Killmonger proposed the idea of sending [[vibranium]] weapons out to [[War Dogs]], giving their resources to other people of African descent to conquer their oppressors. [[File:BP - Killmonger (Arm the Oppressors).png|{{AltFloat}}|Killmonger tells the [[Tribal Council|council]] about his plan]] Setting up a plan, Killmonger declared that African Americans could finally rise up and kill those in power, including their children and anyone else who would take their side, continuing on by saying the public would now know the truth about Wakanda. Sitting back down in his throne, Killmonger listened to [[Okoye]]'s rebuttal with Killmonger's objective, saying that Wakanda had survived for years by fighting only when absolutely necessary. [[File:Killmonger-YouHeardYourOders.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Killmonger gives orders to the [[Tribal Council]]]] While agreeing with Okoye's statement about Wakanda's history, [[W'Kabi]] told Okoye that the world was changing and it would be enough for the outsiders to catch up, saying that it would soon be the conquerors or conquered. As he listened to W'Kabi's logic, Killmonger then accepted his word, told Okoye to go along with her orders and proceeded to take action.{{Ref|BP}} ====[[Battle of Mount Bashenga]]==== [[File:BP Killmonger (Orders Weapons).png|{{AltFloat}}|Killmonger asking [[W'Kabi]] about the mission]] {{Quote|N'Jadaka!"
"Wassup?"
"I never yielded! And as you can see, I am not dead!"
"All that challenge shit is over with! I'm the king now!|[[T'Challa|Black Panther]] and Erik Killmonger|Black Panther (film)}} As ordered by his profound plan, Killmonger prepared shipments of [[Wakanda]]n weapons to be distributed to operatives around the world, so they could have possibly spread it around for other people of African descent in accordance with his [[N'Jobu|father]]'s plan. Walking towards the outside of the aircraft carriers, Killmonger asked [[W'Kabi]] if any spies have been alerted, to which he responded with a yes that some resistance was accepted into their new mission, but the [[War Dogs]] in [[London]], [[New York City]] and [[Hong Kong]] were standing by. [[File:GDUFJgR d.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Killmonger watching [[T'Challa|Black Panther]]'s return]] Notified by the [[Border Tribe]], Killmonger told W'Kabi that the Wakandan army would strike there first and other spies would come around to help. Grabbing a [[Sonic Spear]], Killmonger exemplified how powerful the spear was, explaining that it could stop a tank and leave itself untraceable by metal detectors, declaring the world would know exactly who Wakandans were. Overseeing the ships fleeing, Killmonger saw one of them being shot down and he ran across to see it crash, seeing [[T'Challa]] emerging from the wreckage while wearing his [[Panther Habit]]. [[File:Black Panther OCT17 Trailer 56.png|{{AltFloat}}|Killmonger greets [[T'Challa]]]] While his name was shouted out, Killmonger intrusively greeted T'Challa back into Wakanda. While he demanded that he never yielded and was not killed, Killmonger mockingly rejected his proposal of continuing his challenge and said that he was now the King of Wakanda. Continuing to send aircraft, Killmonger ordered W'Kabi and his army to murder T'Challa, despite him being stopped by [[Okoye]], only to continue sending out his army. [[File:Black Panther OCT17 Trailer 58.png|{{AltFloat}}|Killmonger prepares to fight the [[Dora Milaje]]]] Enraged by his evil nature, and now free to express her opinion since the usurper had just forfeited his legal claim to the throne, Killmonger was yelled at by Okoye, saying that his heart was full of hatred and not fit to be King of Wakanda. As Okoye sent half of the [[Dora Milaje]] to battle with T'Challa, Killmonger was faced with a few of her warriors, as he reached for his dual weapons in order to prepare for his stance. Once he was armed, Killmonger revealed he had taken one of the upgraded Panther Habits for his own use and told the remaining Dora Milaje to fight him. [[File:Black Panther5a8bb48c958b4.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Killmonger fights against the [[Dora Milaje]]]] As Okoye and the Dora Milaje were furiously attacking him, Killmonger managed to withstand their assault and took [[Xoliswa]] captive. As Okoye begged for her to not be sacrificed, she mumbled her last words. Killmonger executed her, making Okoye scream in rage and the Dora Milaje resumed their attack. Dodging all of their blows, Killmonger was caught in between their [[Vibranium Spear]]s, trying to take away his necklace and deactivate his suit. Killmonger released a burst of kinetic energy, sending Okoye and her soldiers away on the battlefield. [[File:Jaguar hi res.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Killmonger breaks [[Shuri]]'s [[Vibranium Gauntlets|gauntlets]]]] When [[Nakia]] and [[Shuri]] arrived at aircraft space, Killmonger noticed them as they began to attack, looking at them and smiling at their expense. As Nakia made the first move, Killmonger dodged and rolled his way out of her range and faced Shuri, getting blasted by her [[Vibranium Gauntlets]]. After getting attacked by Nakia's strikes, Killmonger dodged her attempts to kill him, sliced her thigh and then threw her across the field. He then endured many of Shuri's sonic blasts, continuously until parts of his suit were tearing up. Looking at his spear, Killmonger swiftly destroyed Shuri's gauntlets and subdued her from doing it. [[File:BlackPanther MethodStudios ITW 20.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Killmonger fights against [[T'Challa]] in the air]] Seeing her crawling around, Killmonger cornered Shuri on the ground, mockingly greeting her from her arrival. Preparing for her execution, Killmonger listened to Shuri that he would never be a true King. Ready to stab her with his spear, Killmonger was tackled down by T'Challa, who threw himself once he noticed his sister in peril, falling deeply into the [[Great Mound]]. While falling down, Killmonger broke away from T'Challa's grasp, continuing to fight him and throwing punches until one of them was knocked away from air.{{Ref|BP}} ====Battling Black Panther==== [[File:EKillmonger-Don'tNeedA-SuitToKillYou.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Killmonger realizes his suit is deactivating]] {{Quote|The world took everything away from me! Everything I ever loved! But I'mma make sure we're even. I'mma track down anyone who would even think of being loyal to you! And I'mma put they ass in the dirt, right next to [[Zuri]]!|Erik Killmonger to [[T'Challa]]|Black Panther (film)}} Noticing [[T'Challa]] lying on a railroad, Killmonger focused on his attention to murder him, falling down from another train track and punching him on the ground. Once T'Challa dodged the punch from the ground, Killmonger began to attack him and threw him across the railroad before dodging his panther claws. Pulling each other's punches, both Killmonger and T'Challa released their kinetic energy, resulting in them exploding from impact and lying far away from each other. [[File:EKillmonger-WorldTookEverythingAwayFromMe.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Killmonger arguing against the world]] As Killmonger continued to charge at T'Challa, they were evenly matched for short time in their duel, only for the [[Wakandan Maglev Train]] to be activated, letting sonic stabilizers to disrupt the [[vibranium]] technology in Killmonger's suit. Once they avoided getting hit by the train, Killmonger suddenly noticed that his suit was tearing apart, he began responding to T'Challa, saying he did not need a suit to kill him and that his reign overruling [[Wakanda]] was over. [[File:Black Panther OCT17 Trailer 60.png|{{AltFloat}}|Killmonger attempts to kill [[T'Challa]]]] Despite T'Challa stating he was only becoming just like the people he hated so much, Killmonger responded that he learned from his enemies by beating them at their own position but was told that he had become like his enemies and would destroy the world, including Wakanda. Emotionally, Killmonger argued that the world took everything away from him, everything he ever loved and would make sure that T'Challa and he would be even, by tracking down anyone who would think about being loyal to T'Challa and murdering them, just like he did to [[Zuri]]. [[File:EKillmonger-Stabbed&InPain.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Killmonger tells [[T'Challa]] about his [[N'Jobu|father]]]] Enraged, Killmonger and T'Challa yelled at each other after the argument, continuing to trade blows with each other, they were hit by the train, landing across from the sidelines. Killmonger then noticed his Wakandan spear and began to attack T'Challa with it. With each strike, Killmonger pushed T'Challa toward the edge and pulled the spear towards him. Distracted, Killmonger threatened T'Challa, only to be impaled by his own short spear.{{Ref|BP}} ====Choosing to Die==== [[File:BP - Killmonger Sees The Sunset.png|{{AltFloat}}|Killmonger looks at the [[Sun|sunset]] of [[Wakanda]]]] {{Quote|Maybe we can still heal you..."
"Why, so you can just lock me up? Nah. Just bury me in the ocean with my ancestors who jumped from ships, 'cause they knew death was better than bondage.|[[T'Challa|Black Panther]] and Erik Killmonger|Black Panther (film)}} Aching in pain, Killmonger turned off his mask and took a moment to gasp for air, complimenting [[T'Challa]] on the clever move he used. Killmonger began to look back on a childhood memory of his [[N'Jobu|father]] telling him the [[Sun|sunsets]] on [[Wakanda]] were the most beautiful in the world and the promise that Killmonger would see it one day. Killmonger was carried along by T'Challa to an elevated platform. [[File:BP - Killmonger Dying Breath 1.png|{{AltFloat}}|Killmonger questioning [[T'Challa]]'s final offer]] As they walked over a cliff behind the [[Great Mound]], Killmonger and T'Challa stood near each other and sat down. Looking forward at the sunset, Killmonger was stunningly impressed by how beautiful it was. Having an idea for him, T'Challa told Killmonger he could possibly be healed from his wound that he inflicted on him. Facing toward him, Killmonger asked him why would they heal him, assuming that it would end up with him being imprisoned for life. [[File:EKillmonger-PullingTheSpearAway.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Killmonger pulling the [[Vibranium Zulu Short-Spear|spear]] from his chest]] Declining his offer for a second chance, Killmonger told T'Challa to bury him in the ocean like his ancestors who were brought over by their oppressors and jumped out from their ships, as they knew it was better to die that way than bondage. Earning T'Challa's forgiveness, Killmonger decided to die, painfully removing the [[Vibranium Zulu Short-Spear|spearhead]] from his body, bleeding out and dying peacefully.{{Ref|BP}} ===Legacy=== ====Wakandan Outreach==== {{Quote|I had the courage to do what was necessary to change [[Wakanda]]. How many people like your [[Riri Williams|scientist]] did Wakanda protect before I took the throne? Cowards, those were the [[:Category:Black Panthers|Panthers]] who came before me, and before [[T'Challa]].|N'Jadaka to [[Shuri]]|Black Panther: Wakanda Forever}} Killmonger's brief rule of [[Wakanda]] and his ruling philosophies inspired [[T'Challa]] to bring an end to Wakanda's isolationist practices and to use their resources to help those in need around the world. In addition to creating [[Wakandan International Outreach Centre]]s, including one in Killmonger's hometown of [[Oakland]], [[California]], T'Challa revealed to the world that Wakanda is not a third-world country and that they possess multiple stockpiles of [[vibranium]].{{Ref|BP}} This announcement led to the other nations of [[Earth]] trying to steal vibranium, resulting in tensions between Wakanda and the countries comprised of the [[United Nations]]. [[Namor]], the king of the underwater nation of [[Talokan]], which possessed vibranium, informed Queen [[Ramonda]] and Princess [[Shuri]] that he blamed Wakanda revealing themselves to the world for creating the chain of events that resulted in the surface world nearly discovering Talokan. Namor threatened to attack Wakanda unless they delivered [[Riri Williams]], a scientist who had created a [[Vibranium Detector|vibranium detector]], although Wakanda refused. While in the [[Ancestral Plane]], Killmonger accredited this refusal to the impact of his rule, reminding Shuri that the previous rulers prior to him and T'Challa would have killed Williams for the greater good of Wakanda.{{Ref|BPWF}} ====T'Challa's Death==== Several years after Killmonger's death, [[T'Challa]] fell ill to an unknown disease that might have been cured with additional consumption of [[Heart-Shaped Herb]]s. However, due to Killmonger's orders to burn all of the Heart-Shaped Herbs, the Wakandans were left with no solutions to cure their King, leading to T'Challa succumbing to his illness. As such, [[Shuri]] held Killmonger's actions responsible for her brother's death as well as all subsequent lives that Wakanda lost as a result of there being no Black Panther to protect them.{{Ref|BPWF}} ====Offering Wisdom to Shuri==== [[File:Killmonger (Ancestral Plane).png|{{AltFloat}}|N'Jadaka sees [[Shuri]] in the [[Ancestral Plane]]]] {{Quote|Your [[T'Chaka|father]]? He was a hypocrite. He would have killed that [[Riri Williams|girl]]. Shit, he [[Murder of N'Jobu|killed]] his own [[N'Jobu|brother]]. [[T'Challa]]? He was too noble. He [[Battle at the Hydra Siberian Facility|let]] the [[Helmut Zemo|man]] who [[Bombing of the Vienna International Centre|murdered]] your father live, and here you stand. Are you gonna be noble like your brother? Or take care of business, like me?|N'Jadaka to [[Shuri]]|Black Panther: Wakanda Forever}} Following his death, Killmonger's soul resided in the [[Ancestral Plane]]. [[2025|Nine years later]], [[Shuri]] entered it after having consumed a synthetic [[Heart-Shaped Herb]] to become the new Black Panther in the wake of [[Namor]]'s attack on Wakanda and the death of Queen [[Ramonda]]. Instead of seeing her immediate family, Shuri saw Killmonger sitting in the ruined throne room. Shuri expressed her confusion, to which Killmonger responded that Shuri desired revenge, which is why he was the one visiting her. [[File:Killmonger and Shuri.png|{{AltFloat}}|N'Jadaka advises [[Shuri]] to be more like him]] Killmonger told Shuri that they were no different from each other as they were both motivated by revenge after the death of their loved ones. Despite Shuri's protests, Killmonger claimed that her [[T'Chaka|father]] killed his [[N'Jobu|father]] without hesitation while her brother fought with honor for an old system. He then asked her if she would fight Namor with honor or kill him in cold blood. Ultimately remembering his words, Shuri decided to spare Namor after defeating him in battle, which not only created an alliance between [[Wakanda]] and [[Talokan]], but also prevented Shuri from becoming a vengeful killer similar to how Killmonger was.{{Ref|BPWF}} ==Powers and Abilities== ===Powers=== {{Quote|Allow the [[Heart-Shaped Herb]] to give you the powers of the Black Panther.|[[Sope]] to Erik Killmonger|Black Panther (film)}} *'''[[Heart-Shaped Herb]] Enhancement''': After having consumed the [[Heart-Shaped Herb]], Killmonger's physical attributes were significantly enhanced to levels comparable to a [[:Category:Super Soldier Serum Users|super soldier]]. Unlike the [[Super Soldier Serum]], the effects can be removed by drinking a serum that negates the powers of the herbs for ritualistic combat for a fair fight to become King of [[Wakanda]]. **'''Enhanced Strength''': [[File:BP - DM Tries to Remove Necklace 2.png|thumb|250px|Killmonger overpowering the [[Dora Milaje]]]] The herb endowed Killmonger with strength proportionate to that of his cousin. Following his ingestion of the herb, he lifted a farmer shaman woman by the neck with one hand after she disobeyed his order to burn the remaining herbs. As the Black Panther, Killmonger was able to challenge both [[T'Challa]] and the [[Dora Milaje]] in close combat, proving himself an almost indefatigable fighter. He also threw [[Nakia]] a far distance after he subdued her by slashing her leg with his [[vibranium]] claws. **'''Enhanced Durability''': Even when not wearing his [[Panther Habit]], Killmonger could survive blunt force trauma. However, vibranium weapons could pierce his newly dense flesh, as it is how T'Challa managed to fatally wound him even with a Habit on, with his own blade. **'''Enhanced Speed''': Killmonger could run and move at very fast speeds. He rivaled his competitor, [[T'Challa]] is both running and combat speed. **'''Enhanced Agility''': Killmonger's agility was outstanding enough to allow him to leap great distances with accuracy. He deftly dove over a [[Circular Vibranium Blades|Ring Blade]] hurled at him by [[Nakia]] and swiftly recovered from landing. He could even jump down from a very high platform and land onto the train track during his final fight with T'Challa. **'''Enhanced Stamina''': Killmonger's body could withstand tremendous amounts of fatigue, granting him exceptional endurance and lung capacity. **'''Enhanced Reflexes''': Killmonger's reflexes were quick enough to avoid being hit by the [[Wakandan Maglev Train]] even while dueling T'Challa. **'''Regenerative Healing Factor''': After he ingested the heart-shaped herb, Killmonger quickly healed the cut on his face from T'Challa's [[Vibranium Iklwa Dagger]]. *'''[[Panther Habit]] Capabilities''': After defeating [[T'Challa]] in ritual combat, Killmonger became the Black Panther as well as King of [[Wakanda]]. As the Black Panther, Killmonger obtained a suit called the [[Panther Habit]]. It granted him many abilities on top of his already enhanced structure. The suit is nanotech and is stored in a necklace that is worn by the user. **'''Superhuman Durability''': [[File:BlackPanther MethodStudios ITW 15.jpg|thumb|250px|Killmonger withstands [[Shuri]]'s energy blasts]] While wearing the Habit, Killmonger's durability was increased to superhuman levels thanks to its [[vibranium]] composition, which allowed him to withstand bullets and explosions while being completely unfazed. He was able to sustain damage from the [[Dora Milaje]]'s [[vibranium]]-bladed weapons, blasts from [[Shuri]]'s gauntlets, attacks from [[Nakia]]'s [[Circular Vibranium Blades]], [[T'Challa|Black Panther]]'s kinetic energy pulses and even an immense fall into the [[Great Mound]] after T'Challa tackled him off of Shuri. He even withstood being knocked back by the [[Wakandan Maglev Train]] and quickly got back up. **'''Claw Retraction''': The [[Panther Habit]] came with [[vibranium]] claws that retracted from the nails. He used these to great effect in the [[Battle of Mount Bashenga]] against [[T'Challa]]. **'''Kinetic Energy Absorption''': The habit can store kinetic energy, which Killmonger could then release in a pulse of golden energy. When the [[Dora Milaje]] turned on him, Killmonger was able to break the energy lock formed by their [[Vibranium Spear]]s with a pulse, throwing them back several meters. The kinetic energy stored within the habit can also be used to add more force behind Killmonger's blows, allowing him to increase the strength of his punches and kicks. ===Abilities=== {{Quote|I don't need a suit to kill you.|Erik Killmonger to [[T'Challa]]|Black Panther (film)}} *'''Master Martial Artist''': [[File:Black Panther (film) BPvsKillmonger-Subway.jpg|thumb|250px|Killmonger fighting against [[T'Challa|Black Panther]]]] Due to his lifelong rigorous training as a SEAL and Black Ops mercenary, Killmonger was an extremely skilled and formidable hand-to-hand combatant and martial artist, making him one of the most powerful and best fighters on the planet. His fighting style included martial arts disciplines from the Navy Seals, such as Boxing, Judo, Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu, Krav Maga, and Muay Thai, and also supplements with Taekwondo, Silat, Tiger style Kung Fu, Kali, and Zulu Stick Fighting. Killmonger's skill was great enough that in their first duel, in which both of them had no superhuman physical abilities, he was able to fight reasonably evenly with T'Challa and even ultimately defeat him, although his victory was aided due to the fact T'Challa was currently conflicted from the recent knowledge of his father's misdeeds and unwillingness to deliver a fatal blow. After drinking the heart-shaped herb, Killmonger became even more formidable due to gaining superhuman physical powers, to the point that while wearing the upgraded Panther Habit, he could fight off many members of Dora Milaje and kill one before being subdued and quickly outfight Nakia despite her having aid from Shuri using her gauntlets to stun him. In their final fight, with T'Challa fighting to kill now, Killmonger proved able to stalemate and even at times dominate T'Challa wearing his Black Panther Habit, only being beaten due to T'Challa exposing his suits' by being away from the sonic fields of the [[Wakandan Maglev Train]]. *'''Sword Mastery''': [[File:BP Teaser Trailer 51.png|thumb|250px|Killmonger fighting [[T'Challa]] with his sword]] Killmonger was extremely skilled at using a sword in combat, being able to use it to go up against T'Challa in their duel for the throne and even beat him, and later easily defeat many members of [[Dora Milaje]] and Nakia by himself. He killed [[Xoliswa]] with the [[Vibranium Ikakalaka Sword]]. *'''Spear Mastery''': Killmonger was extremely skilled at using spears in combat, being able to use a short spear to go up against [[T'Challa]] in their duel for the throne and even beat him. He also defeated the [[Dora Milaje]] and [[Nakia]] using spears. He killed [[Zuri]] with the [[Spear of Bashenga]] as well. *'''Master Marksman''': As a former American Black Ops mercenary, Killmonger was an extremely skilled marksman with incredibly accurate with firearms. He was able to wield both high caliber assault rifles, as well as handguns with great accuracy. This was best demonstrated when he gunned down several guards in quick succession when rescuing Klaue and later when he betrayed Klaue, he was able to shoot him through a window after finding his position just by where Klaue's gunfire tried to hit him. He also shot and killed [[Limbani]] and [[Linda (Mercenary)|Linda]]. *'''Master Acrobat''': Killmonger's immense agility made him an extraordinary gymnast and acrobat, which allowed him to combat Black Panther on equal grounds. {{Quote|He racked up confirmed kills like it was a video game.|[[Everett Ross]]|Black Panther (film)}} *'''Master Assassin''': [[File:N'Jadaka.png|thumb|250px|Killmonger showing his kill-count markings]] Due to his intensive experience as a Navy SEAL and mercenary in an American Black Ops Ghost unit, Killmonger was extremely skilled in assassination techniques, as well as espionage, intelligence gathering, infiltration and sabotage. He gained the nickname "Killmonger" due to his substantial kill-count which, according to [[Everett Ross]], he "racked up like a video game." {{Quote|I learn from my enemies. Beat them at they own game.|Erik Killmonger to [[T'Challa]]|Black Panther (film)}} *'''Master Tactician''': Killmonger was a proficient strategist, able to formulate and execute cunning strategic plans. He was able to find a Wakandan weapon made of [[vibranium]] within the [[Museum of Great Britain]] and, with his group, helped [[Ulysses Klaue]] steal the weapon. When [[T'Challa]] finally managed to capture Klaue after thirty years of pursuit, Killmonger and his group were able to strategically save him. {{Quote|Ungubani?"Translates frm Xhosa to: "''Who are you?''"
"Indingu N'Jadaka! Unyanka [[N'Jobu]]!Translates from Xhosa to: "''I am N'Jadaka, son of Prince [[N'Jobu]]!''"|[[River Tribe Elder]] and Erik Killmonger|Black Panther (film)}} *'''Bilingualism''': Killmonger was fluent in his native English, and was taught to speak {{WPS|Xhosa language|Xhosa}} by his father. ==Equipment== ===Weapons=== *'''{{WPS|Springfield Armory 911|Springfield Armory 1911 Loaded MC Operator}}''': [[File:Killmonger-1911-Loaded-MC-Operator.png|thumb|250px|Killmonger arming himself with a handgun]] Killmonger used this handgun with a threaded barrel when he betrayed [[Ulysses Klaue]], quickly killing [[Limbani]] and shooting at [[Linda (Mercenary)|Linda]] when Klaue took her as hostage. He pursued Klaue and shot him in the chest, killing him with another bullet to the head once he revealed his Wakandan heritage.{{Ref|BP}} *'''{{WPS|BCM Recce-14|BCM Recce-14 KMR-A13}} with {{WPS|M203 grenade launcher|LMT M203 2003}}''': While donning his [[Dogon Mask]], Killmonger carried this rifle outfitted with a grenade launcher in order to rescue [[Ulysses Klaue]]. He opened fire on the CIA site, hitting [[Everett Ross]] in the back with a bullet when the latter was protecting [[Nakia]]. He kept shooting while escaping, firing a grenade at [[T'Challa|Black Panther]] in order to prevent him from pursuing. *'''{{WPS|M67 grenade|M67 Grenade}}''': Killmonger used this grenade to cover their exit from the [[CIA South Korean Black Site]], rescuing [[Ulysses Klaue]] from their imprisonment by the [[CIA]]. *'''[[Vibranium Zulu Short-Spear]]''': [[File:BP - Killmonger Ready to Kill the DM.png|thumb|250px|Killmonger with his spear and sword]] Killmonger wielded a spear that he had broken down to the size of a small blade, which he wielded as a knife-like weapon. He used it to combat T'Challa in order to fight for the throne. He kept the weapon as a trophy and would wield it again as he was fighting the [[Dora Milaje]] while they surrounded him with their [[Vibranium Spear|spears]]. During his final fight, T'Challa stole Killmonger's spear and stabbed him in the heart to kill him. *'''[[Vibranium Ikakalaka Sword]]''': Killmonger wielded a sword that he had owned in order to combat T'Challa in order to fight for the throne. He kept the weapon as a trophy and would wield it again as he was fighting the [[Dora Milaje]] while they surrounded him with their [[Vibranium Spear|spears]]. *'''[[Spear of Bashenga]]''': Prepared to kill [[T'Challa]] in the [[Coronation of Erik Killmonger|ceremonial duel]] on [[Warrior Falls]], Killmonger's final strike was blocked by [[Zuri]] who asked to kill him in T'Challa's place. However, Killmonger replied that he would kill them both and mortally stabbed Zuri with his own spear. ===Other Equipment=== *'''[[Wakandan Royal Ring]]''': The royal ring worn by Killmonger is one of a set originally worn by [[Azzuri]], passed down to [[T'Chaka]] and [[N'Jobu]], and later their sons. Killmonger obtained the ring following N'Jobu's death in Oakland. *'''[[War Dogs Tattoo]]''': In his youth, Killmonger was given a War Dog tattoo, a symbol given to [[Wakanda]]ns to mark them as members of the [[War Dogs]]. {{Quote|You're not telling me that that's [[vibranium]] too, are you?"
"Nah, I'm just feeling it.|[[Ulysses Klaue]] and Erik Killmonger|Black Panther (film)}} *'''[[Dogon Mask]]''': [[File:BP Teaser Trailer 26.png|thumb|250px|Killmonger wears a traditional African mask]] Killmonger stole a ceremonial mask from the [[Museum of Great Britain]] that he could use to conceal his identity. Killmonger wore the mask when he rescued Klaue from questioning. *'''Bulletproof Vest''': Killmonger wore this bulletproof vest when operating on missions, most notably when breaking Klaue out of captivity. *'''[[Kimoyo Beads]]''': The Kimoyo beads are pieces of advanced communication technology adopted by the Wakandans from [[vibranium]]. Killmonger used the Kimoyo beads to prepare the shipments of Wakandan weapons to be distributed to operatives around the world, so they could have possibly spread it to other people of African descent. *'''[[Panther Habit]]''': This suit is one of two upgraded versions of the [[Panther Habit]], which Killmonger took for his own use. The Habit is made from Wakandan nanotechnology that is emitted from a ceremonial golden necklace worn by the user, able to mentally command the Habit to manifest around themselves. The Habit can emit stored kinetic energy as an omnidirectional concussive wave to repel opponents close to the wearer and can be used to propel the wearer off a surface. The suit's gloves also contained retractable vibranium claws. ==Facilities== *'''[[N'Jobu's Safehouse]]''': [[File:Erik-Ancestral Plane.png|thumb|250px|Killmonger views his childhood home]] Born in [[Oakland]], Stevens lived with his father [[N'Jobu]] at his apartment and own safe house in [[California]] where he found N'Jobu's [[Wakanda]]n property. As Stevens played basketball outside of the house with other kids from the block, he suddenly saw the [[Royal Talon Fighter]] above them. He rushed back to the apartment where he found N'Jobu killed by [[T'Chaka]], and cried, holding his father's body. Many years later, when Killmonger became the King of Wakanda, he consumed the [[Heart-Shaped Herb|heart-shaped herb]] and woke up at the [[Ancestral Plane|Ancestral Plane]]. There, he found himself in the recreation of his old house in Oakland where he reunited with N'Jobu's spirit. *'''[[Citadel]]''': Defeating [[T'Challa]] in the [[Coronation of Erik Killmonger|ceremonial duel]], Killmonger became the King of [[Wakanda]], as he always wanted. Taking the rulership of Wakanda and the mantle of the [[:Category:Black Panthers|Black Panther]], Killmonger was granted with the throne in the [[Citadel]]. In front of the [[Tribal Council]], Killmonger stated that they cannot stay dark anymore and Wakanda needs to go offensive against the outside world. ==Relationships== {{Col-begin}}{{Col-2}} ===Family=== *[[Golden Tribe]] **[[Bashenga]] † - Former Lover and Victim *[[Limbani]] † - Former Associate and Victim *[[Dora Milaje]] - Former Subordinates **[[Okoye]] **[[Ayo]] **[[Xoliswa]] † - Victim **[[Nomble]] **[[Yama]] *[[Tribal Council]] **[[M'Kathu]] **[[River Tribe Elder]] **[[Merchant Tribe Elder]] **[[Zawavari]] *[[Jabari Tribe]] **[[M'Baku]] *[[Sope]] {{Col-end}} ==Appearances== |-|Chronological= In chronological order: *{{Appearance|EoW104}} (mentioned) *{{Appearance|BP}} (flashback) *{{Appearance|BP}} **{{Appearance|EoW104}} **{{Appearance|BPAN}} **{{Appearance|MDCCBP}} *{{Appearance|BPWF}} |-|Narrative= In narrative order: *{{Appearance|BP}} (flashback) *{{Appearance|BP}} **{{Appearance|EoW104}} **{{Appearance|BPAN}} **{{Appearance|MDCCBP}} *{{Appearance|BPWF}} *{{Appearance|EoW104}} (mentioned) ==Trivia== *In the comics, '''Erik Killmonger''', originally known as '''N'Jadaka''', was a [[Wakanda|Wakandan]] native who was exiled after his father was forced to work for [[Ulysses Klaue]] and attempted to overthrow [[T'Challa]] from the throne upon his return. *Killmonger's scarification markings on his torso and arms resemble that of the {{WPS|Mursi}} and {{WPS|Surma}} tribes. *The mask Killmonger wears when rescuing Klaue is a reference to his masked armor he wears in the comics during a fight with Black Panther wearing the Thrice Blessed Armor. *Killmonger is the second main antagonist to be killed by the titular character(s) of the film in which he appears, the first being [[Ronan the Accuser]]. ==Behind the Scenes== *[[Michael B. Jordan]] noted the character's dreadlocks hairstyle was a way ''"to stay true to the original character ['s long hair] as much as possible and make it as modern as we could."''[http://www.mtv.com/news/3026870/michael-b-jordan-black-panther-villain-unapologetically-who-he-is/ MICHAEL B. JORDAN'S BLACK PANTHER VILLAIN IS 'UNAPOLOGETICALLY WHO HE IS'] *[[Michael B. Jordan]] described Killmonger as ''"very strategic, thoughtful. He's very patient. Very well skilled, trained to a T. He works very hard, and he feels what he's doing is right."''[http://au.ign.com/articles/2017/07/24/comic-con-2017-what-motivates-villain-killmonger-in-black-panther COMIC-CON 2017: WHAT MOTIVATES VILLAIN KILLMONGER IN BLACK PANTHER] *[[Michael B. Jordan]] compared Killmonger and [[T'Challa]]'s relationship to the X-Men characters [[Magneto]] and [[Professor X]]. He also compared his performance to {{WPS|Michael Fassbender}}'s take on [[Magneto]] in the {{WPS|X-Men (film series)|''X-Men'' movie franchise}}, and to {{WPS|Heath Ledger}}'s {{WPS|Joker (The Dark Knight)|Joker}} in ''{{WPS|The Dark Knight}}''.[https://www.empireonline.com/movies/black-panther/black-panther-empire-exclusive-newsstand-cover-revealed/ Black Panther: Empire's Exclusive Newsstand Cover Revealed] *Erik Killmonger's armor bears a resemblance to {{WPS|Vegeta}}'s costume from the ''{{WPS|Dragon Ball Z}}'' anime series, which the actor Michael B. Jordan is a fan of.[https://www.cbr.com/black-panther-killmonger-vegeta-costume/ "Black Panther's Killmonger May Have Borrowed His Costume From Vegeta"] *To prepare for the role of Erik Killmonger, [[Michael B. Jordan]] wrote a diary from Killmonger's perspective.[https://www.cbr.com/black-panther-jordan-killmonger-diary/ Black Panther's Jordan Wrote a Diary From Killmonger's Perspective] *[[Khalid Ghajji]] and [[Aaron Toney]] were stunt doubles for [[Michael B. Jordan]] in the role of Erik Killmonger. *Though rumors have persisted that [[Michael B. Jordan]] would be returning as Erik Killmonger in ''[[Black Panther: Wakanda Forever]]'', communicating with [[T'Challa]] from the [[Ancestral Plane]], [[Kevin Feige]] has affirmed that those rumors are false.[https://www.bet.com/celebrities/exclusives/kevin-feige-marvel-interview-spider-man.html We Asked Marvel Studios President If ‘Into The Spider-Verse’ Had Him Shook And If Nick Fury Will Ever Make It To Wakanda] He does, however, appear in the film where he communicates with Shuri instead. ==References== {{Gallery|Erik Killmonger/Gallery}} {{Wq|Erik Killmonger}} {{Reflist}} {{Succession box |before = [[T'Challa]] |title = King of [[Wakanda]]
